repositories.brief_summary: Week 10 of 2026 saw significant activity in the OCaml compiler development, with 15 PRs merged, 14 new PRs opened, and several important bug fixes ...
repositories.new_features_summary: Five improvements including Fun.todo function, warning 16 fixes, UTF-8 optimization, continuation efficiency, and type system fixes
repositories.new_features:
- Added [#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14525) `Fun.todo` function to signal unimplemented functionality, with code location support and optional `todo` alert for detecting remaining placeholder code (merged [#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14525) by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), closing long-standing issue [#13072](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13072))
- Improved [#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14512) warning 16 (unerasable-optional-argument) detection to properly handle module-dependent function types (`Tfunctor`) introduced in OCaml 5.5 (merged [#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14512) by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), fixing regression [#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622))
- Optimized [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14620) UTF-8 lexeme processing in the compiler for better performance (merged [#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14620) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- Made continuation objects more efficient by reducing from two fields to one in [#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14469) (backported to trunk via [#14613](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14613))
- Fixed [#14604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14604) uncaught Cannot_apply exception when mixing polymorphic types and unboxed constructors (merged [#14604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14604) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), fixing [#14603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14603))

repositories.discussions_summary: Runtime cleanup debates, programmable patterns proposal, and multiple OCaml 5.5 regression reports
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Runtime Cleanup**: Extensive discussion on [#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) about segfaults and timeouts in parallel tests with cleanup-at-exit mode, exploring fundamental design questions about thread cancellation and resource cleanup. The debate centers on whether cleanup-at-exit should attempt to stop other domains/threads or require well-written programs to join all threads before exit.
- **Feature Request**: [#14627](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14627) proposing programmable patterns for OCaml to enable pattern matching on abstract types without exposing constructors, similar to Haskell's pattern synonyms and F#'s active patterns. This long-standing gap between pattern matching and data abstraction could significantly improve library ergonomics.
- **Type System Regression**: [#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622) reporting warning 16 incorrectly raised with GADTs and first-class modules in 5.5 (closed by [#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14512)). The regression was caused by modular explicits introducing `Tfunctor` type constructor without updating warning detection code.
- **Type System Regression**: [#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630) about signature inference failure with recursive functions and first-class modules in 5.5, likely to be fixed by [#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14537)
- **Type System Regression**: [#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629) about local open changing typechecker behavior for optional argument elimination in 5.5
- **Performance**: [#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14621) identifying tests taking too long on AppVeyor CI (some over 185 seconds, including forbidden.ml at 185s), suggesting need to reduce test iteration counts
- **Documentation**: [#14628](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14628) requesting addition of `iarray` to built-in types in manual (opened and closed same week)
- **Stdlib**: [#14619](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14619) questioning why List.concat/flatten is not tail-recursive
repositories.trends_summary: OCaml 5.5 regressions with Tfunctor, active type system work, runtime cleanup focus, and performance optimization
repositories.emerging_trends:
Multiple OCaml 5.5 regressions identified related to modular explicits and the new `Tfunctor` type constructor, highlighting the need for systematic audits when introducing new type constructors
Continued active work on the complex keep-expansion type system refactoring ([#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/11648)), with cross-review between Jacques Garrigue and Stefan Muenzel
Growing focus on runtime cleanup and domain management issues, particularly around cleanup-at-exit mode and proper resource management
Documentation improvements continue with missing @since annotations being added and API documentation being expanded
Performance optimization work focusing on UTF-8 processing and compiler internals

repositories.brief_summary: LSP server enhancements with range formatting, type location, and refactoring extraction capabilities
repositories.new_features_summary: Range formatting support, locate types custom request, and refactor extraction query added
repositories.new_features:
- **Range formatting support** via `textDocument/rangeFormatting` request enables formatting selected code regions while preserving surrounding context and indentation ([#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591))
- **Locate types custom request** (`ocamllsp/locateTypes`) returns tree representation of all types composing an expression's type, including support for tracking type references across the codebase ([#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584))
- **Refactor extract custom query** (`ocamllsp/refactorExtract`) provides commands to extract arbitrary code regions into fresh let bindings with automatic name generation ([#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545))
- **Signature help capability** now properly advertised in server capabilities table in README, confirming support for `textDocument/signatureHelp` ([#1592](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1592))
repositories.activity_summary: Jump functionality improvements under review, while dependency updates and refactoring work continue
repositories.activity:
- **Jump functionality enhancement** opened by [PixieDust](https://github.com/PizieDust) implements performance improvements by querying all possible jump targets at once instead of recursively checking each, showing response time improvements from 523ms to 244ms ([#1461](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1461))
- **Refactor extract code action** opened by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) replaces previous broken implementation with proper Merlin-based extraction for code actions, though currently shows merge conflicts ([#1546](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1546))
- **Flake lock updates** automated by GitHub Actions continue with updates to merlin5_3 and nixpkgs dependencies ([#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437))
- Attempted jump refactoring by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) was closed without merging ([#1593](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1593))

repositories.brief_summary: Package management improvements with tar extraction fixes, Windows cache fixes, and memo deadlock resolution
repositories.new_features_summary: Tar extraction for OpenBSD, Windows cache hard links, relative path fixes in %{bin:..}, and diff action improvements
repositories.new_features:
- **Tar extraction compatibility** on systems with non-auto-detecting tar implementations (e.g., OpenBSD) by passing explicit decompression flags (`-z` for gzip, `-j` for bzip2) with clear error messages for unsupported formats like XZ and LZMA ([#13688](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13688) fixes [#10123](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10123))
- **Windows Dune cache** now uses hard links for better performance ([#13714](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13714)) and correctly handles renames onto read-only files during deduplication ([#13713](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13713))
- **Relative path handling** in `%{bin:..}` and `%{bin-available:..}` now interprets paths correctly relative to the dune file location ([#13712](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13712) fixes [#9564](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9564))
- **Diff action improvements** for handling empty files and missing files, now correctly passing `/dev/null` to `--diff-command` instead of non-existent paths ([#13696](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13696)) and better diff output on patch-back-source-tree failures ([#13697](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13697))
- **Memo system deadlock fix** that checks for self-dependency cycles before blocking on ivar, resolving infinite loop in cycle checking ([#13710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13710) fixes [#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771))
- **Package describe workspace** now skips libraries that cannot be resolved instead of failing completely ([#13724](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13724) fixes [#12997](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12997))
- **Package management fixes** including filtering unknown variables in depexts ([#13729](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13729) fixes [#13699](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13699)), ensuring dev tool lock dir is built before loading ([#13648](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13648)), fixing extra files with autolocking ([#13595](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13595) fixes [#12851](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12851)), and making `dune show depexts` print to stdout ([#13717](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13717))
- **Rev store optimization** to only negotiate with relevant tips instead of all refs ([#13698](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13698) fixes [#13671](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13671))
- **Dune RPC error handling** now properly handles errors from server requests ([#13709](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13709))

- **Windows platform improvements** by [Roven Gabriel](https://github.com/Nevor) with hard link support in cache ([#13714](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13714)) and read-only file rename handling ([#13713](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13713))
- **Core build system fixes** by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) including diff action missing files ([#13696](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13696)), patch-back-source-tree diff output ([#13697](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13697)), RPC error handling ([#13709](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13709)), and error message refactoring ([#13739](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13739))
- **Memo and describe improvements** by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) fixing self-dependency cycle detection ([#13710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13710)), workspace description with missing libraries ([#13724](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13724)), relative path interpretation ([#13712](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13712)), and cache refactoring ([#13711](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13711))
- **Testing and CI infrastructure** updates by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) fixing dune-action-trace dependency bounds ([#13730](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13730)), skipping install tests ([#13736](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13736)), and mirage CI dependencies ([#13720](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13720)), plus [Will Thomas](https://github.com/Durbatuluk1701) setting default Git configuration for tests ([#13727](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13727))
- **Dependency maintenance** with dependabot updating GitHub Actions including upload-artifact from 6 to 7 ([#13705](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13705)) and attest-build-provenance from 3 to 4 ([#13704](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13704)), and [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) updating nix flakes ([#13706](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13706))
- **Test coverage** expanded with transitive PPX cookie test by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) ([#13686](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13686)) and memo deadlock reproduction by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro) ([#13707](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13707), [#13722](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13722))

repositories.trends_summary: Package management robustness with platform-specific fixes and long-standing bug resolution
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Package management robustness** continues as a focus area with comprehensive work on archive extraction, lock file handling, depext filtering, and autolocking across multiple PRs by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Windows platform support** receiving dedicated attention with cache improvements enabling hard links and proper file handling on Windows by [Roven Gabriel](https://github.com/Nevor)
- **Long-standing bug resolution** with closure of issues from 2022-2024 including infinite loop in cycle checking ([#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771)), OpenBSD tar compatibility ([#10123](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10123)), and relative path handling ([#9564](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9564))
- **Testing infrastructure improvements** with focus on CI correctness, dependency bounds validation, and better test isolation through default Git configuration
- **Cross-platform compatibility** addressing OpenBSD tar implementations, Windows cache behavior, and preparing for broader archive format support (XZ, LZMA)

repositories.brief_summary: Intensive backend optimization and type system refinement with 48 merged PRs from 23 contributors
repositories.new_features_summary: Register allocation improvements, unboxed types API refinement, reaper robustness fixes
repositories.new_features:
- Register allocation and backend optimizations:
  - [#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514) improved register allocation by only reloading (not spilling) loop invariant registers by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - [#5535](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5535) optimized shift operations by eliminating unnecessary sign extensions on shift amounts by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - [#5550](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5550) avoided redundant sign extensions in Cmm builtins returning int32 by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - [#5542](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5542) fixed loop optimization bug in the split pass by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)
- Unboxed types and type system improvements:
  - [#5556](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5556) replaced array block index syntax with explicit %unsafe_array_idx primitives, localizing unsafety to creation by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)
  - [#5328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5328) optimized block index bounds checking by shifting instead of masking by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)
  - [#5564](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5564) fixed missing mode-crossing in functional record update by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
  - [#5555](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5555) fixed inlining bug in classic mode with void parameters by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
- Reaper robustness and debugging improvements:
  - [#5547](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5547) prevented failure when rewriting types for value slots with no sources by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - [#5540](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5540) prevented fatal errors when unused symbol fields are unboxed by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - [#5541](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5541) refactored let-bound variable rewriting to reduce code complexity by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - [#5549](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5549) replaced assert false with informative fatal errors by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
  - [#5528](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5528) fixed ordering of cannot_unbox0 rules by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - [#5391](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5391) fixed type rewriting to include constant types by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- New compilation infrastructure:
  - [#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5430) added static computation framework with new tlambda→slambda→rawlambda pipeline by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)
  - [#5097](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5097) made name mangling scheme configurable via configure options by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)
  - [#5522](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5522) exposed Domain.max_domain_count for reliable multicore detection by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
- Testing and debugging infrastructure:
  - [#5409](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5409) added comprehensive CFI/DWARF backtrace testing with gdb validation by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
  - [#5489](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5489) expanded codegen test coverage for intrinsics and builtins (3300+ lines) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)

- Synchronized with upstream **OCaml compiler version 5.2.0minus-32** including updates to jkind system, mode analysis, and type system internals ([#224](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/224))
repositories.activity_summary: Four new signature help PRs opened, two PRs merged improving type recovery and compiler sync
repositories.activity:
- Four **new PRs opened** by [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) for signature help improvements including tests for labelled-only arguments ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)), fixing optional argument suggestions ([#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216)), addressing `let..in` binding issues ([#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223)), and adding test cases for unfinished `let...in` bindings ([#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215))

- **Compiler synchronization** completed by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merging 5.2.0minus-32 changes affecting 107 files with updates to jkind system including new jkind_axis module, mode analysis refinements, and type system internals ([#224](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/224))
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on type recovery approach and upstream coordination
repositories.notable_discussions:

- **Signature help downstream coordination** with [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) informing [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) about fixes merged in upstream [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032) and requesting review ([#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216))
- **CI failures** on labelled arguments tests discussed between [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) due to unrelated file changes, resolved by rebasing onto main ([#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205))
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on signature help and upstream synchronization with ocaml/merlin
repositories.emerging_trends:

group_summaries.brief_summary: Package management improvements, LSP refactoring features, and OCaml 5.5 support work across tooling ecosystem
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune tar extraction, Windows cache, refactor-extract commands in Merlin and LSP, range formatting, and type location queries
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Dune tar extraction compatibility** on systems with non-auto-detecting tar implementations (e.g., OpenBSD) by passing explicit decompression flags in [ocaml/dune#13688](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13688) by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- **Dune Windows cache** now uses hard links for better performance ([ocaml/dune#13714](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13714)) and correctly handles renames onto read-only files during deduplication ([ocaml/dune#13713](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13713)) by [Roven Gabriel](https://github.com/Nevor)
- **Dune relative path handling** in `%{bin:..}` and `%{bin-available:..}` now interprets paths correctly relative to the dune file location in [ocaml/dune#13712](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13712) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **Dune memo system deadlock fix** checking for self-dependency cycles before blocking on ivar in [ocaml/dune#13710](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13710) by [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro), resolving issue from 2022 ([ocaml/dune#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771))
- **Merlin experimental refactor-extract-region command** for extracting code regions into fresh let bindings in [ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/1948) by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)
- **OCaml-LSP range formatting support** via `textDocument/rangeFormatting` request enables formatting selected code regions while preserving context in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591) by [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian)
- **OCaml-LSP locate types custom request** (`ocamllsp/locateTypes`) returns tree representation of all types composing an expression's type in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584) by [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw)
- **OCaml-LSP refactor extract custom query** (`ocamllsp/refactorExtract`) provides commands to extract arbitrary code regions into fresh let bindings in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545) by [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- **Dune** led activity with extensive package management improvements including tar extraction fixes ([ocaml/dune#13688](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13688)), Windows cache enhancements ([ocaml/dune#13714](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13714), [ocaml/dune#13713](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/13713)), and resolution of long-standing bugs from 2022-2024
- **OCaml-LSP** delivered new refactoring capabilities with range formatting ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1591](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1591)), type location queries ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584)), and extract commands ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545))
- **Merlin** merged experimental refactor-extract-region command ([ocaml/merlin#1948](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/pull/1948)) enabling code extraction into let bindings
- **OCaml 5.5 support** work initiated in [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2195) and requested for [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)
- **Opam** received Windows compatibility fixes ([ocaml/opam#6862](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6862)) and UI action ordering improvements ([ocaml/opam#6864](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6864))
- **Cross-repository refactoring work** coordinated between [Tim ats](https://github.com/Tim-ats-d) in Merlin and OCaml-LSP for code extraction features
- **No activity** reported in [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind) or [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) repositories

group_summaries.brief_summary: 48 PRs merged across compiler and tooling with focus on backend optimization and type system refinement
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Register allocation improvements, unboxed types API refinement, type recovery fixes, compiler sync to 5.2.0minus-32
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Backend optimization in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**:
  - Improved register allocation by only reloading (not spilling) loop invariant registers in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - Optimized shift operations by eliminating unnecessary sign extensions on shift amounts in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5535](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5535) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - Avoided redundant sign extensions in Cmm builtins returning int32 in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5550](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5550) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
  - Fixed loop optimization bug in the split pass in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5542](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5542) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)
- **Unboxed types and type system improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**:
  - Replaced array block index syntax with explicit %unsafe_array_idx primitives in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5556](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5556) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)
  - Optimized block index bounds checking by shifting instead of masking in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5328) by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa)
  - Fixed missing mode-crossing in functional record update in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5564](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5564) by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
  - Fixed inlining bug in classic mode with void parameters in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5555](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5555) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
- **Reaper robustness improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**:
  - Prevented failure when rewriting types for value slots with no sources in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5547](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5547) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - Prevented fatal errors when unused symbol fields are unboxed in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5540](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5540) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - Refactored let-bound variable rewriting to reduce code complexity in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5541](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5541) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - Replaced assert false with informative fatal errors in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5549](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5549) by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell)
  - Fixed ordering of cannot_unbox0 rules in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5528](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5528) by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs)
  - Fixed type rewriting to include constant types in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5391](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5391) by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp)
- **New compilation infrastructure in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**:
  - Added static computation framework with new tlambda→slambda→rawlambda pipeline in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5430) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)
  - Made name mangling scheme configurable via configure options in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5097](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5097) by [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist)
  - Exposed Domain.max_domain_count for reliable multicore detection in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5522](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5522) by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
- **Testing and debugging infrastructure in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**:
  - Added comprehensive CFI/DWARF backtrace testing with gdb validation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5409](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5409) by [Stephen Dolan](https://github.com/stedolan)
  - Expanded codegen test coverage for intrinsics and builtins (3300+ lines) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5489](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5489) by [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet)
- **Type recovery improvements in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**:
  - Improved type recovery in function applications with argument type mismatches in local opens, fixing "Not in environment" errors in [oxcaml/merlin#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
  - Synchronized with upstream OCaml compiler version 5.2.0minus-32 including updates to jkind system, mode analysis, and type system internals in [oxcaml/merlin#224](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/224) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923)
group_summaries.activity_summary: 22 new PRs opened in oxcaml/oxcaml, 4 signature help PRs in oxcaml/merlin, 1 package publication issue in oxcaml/opam-repository
group_summaries.activity:
- **New PRs in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: 22 pull requests opened including slambda fracturing implementation in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5544) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr), AVX2 gather instructions fix in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5567](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5567) by [Max Slater](https://github.com/TheNumbat), abstract kinds for recursive modules in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5517](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5517) by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin), and CFG terminator simplification series ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5545](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5545), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5546](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5546), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5562](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5562)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc)
- **5.4 merge progress in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: Continued conflict resolution with .mli files in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5557](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5557), bytecomp in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5426](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5426), and tools/ in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5503](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5503) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) and [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon)

- **Package publication in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository)**: [Nirnay Roy](https://github.com/nirnayroy) opened [oxcaml/opam-repository#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) asking about publishing the nx-oxcaml package from the raven project to opam
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Upstream-downstream synchronization**: [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) demonstrated workflow where fixes are implemented in [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) first (as seen in [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032)) and then downstreamed to oxcaml/merlin, maintaining consistency between forks as discussed in [oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) and [oxcaml/merlin#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222)
- **Compiler version alignment**: [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) synchronized with OCaml compiler version 5.2.0minus-32 in [oxcaml/merlin#224](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/224) to stay aligned with [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) type system changes including jkind system updates and mode analysis refinements
- **Package ecosystem development**: [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) received inquiry in [oxcaml/opam-repository#35](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/35) about publishing packages that depend on [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml), indicating growing downstream adoption
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Type recovery implementation strategy in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: [Ulysse](https://github.com/voodoos) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) discussed the fix for [oxcaml/merlin#222](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/222), discovering the solution was already implemented upstream in commit 732bd24, leading to cherry-picking the upstream approach instead of the original implementation
- **Signature help downstream coordination in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) informed [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) in [oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216) about fixes merged in upstream [ocaml/merlin#2032](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2032) and requested review
- **CI troubleshooting in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) and [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) resolved CI failures in [oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205) on labelled arguments tests caused by unrelated file changes, fixed by rebasing onto main
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Backend performance focus in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: Series of optimizations targeting elimination of unnecessary operations including sign extensions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5535](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5535), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5550](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5550)), redundant register spills ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514)), and improved bounds checking ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5328))
- **Unboxed types API maturation in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: Refinement of primitive APIs with explicit %unsafe_array_idx in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5556](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5556), better safety boundaries between safe and unsafe operations, and mode-crossing fixes in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5564](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5564)
- **Reaper robustness improvements in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: Systematic bug fixing including error handling ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5547](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5547), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5540](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5540), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5549](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5549)), type rewriting ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5391](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5391), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5528](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5528)), and code simplification ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5541](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5541))
- **Static evaluation infrastructure in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml)**: New compilation pipeline (tlambda→slambda→rawlambda) introduced in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5430) with follow-up slambda fracturing in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5544) by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr)
- **Test coverage expansion across repositories**: Comprehensive CFI/DWARF testing in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5409](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5409), codegen test suite (3300+ lines) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5489](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5489), and signature help test cases in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205), [oxcaml/merlin#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215))
- **Signature help refinement in [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)**: Focused development from [Charlène_Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo) addressing edge cases in labelled arguments ([oxcaml/merlin#205](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/205)), optional parameters ([oxcaml/merlin#216](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/216)), and incomplete let bindings ([oxcaml/merlin#215](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/215), [oxcaml/merlin#223](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/223))

group_summaries.brief_summary: OCaml compiler saw 15 PRs merged and 14 new PRs opened, with focus on type system fixes, runtime improvements, and OCaml 5.5 regressions
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Five improvements: Fun.todo function, warning 16 fixes, UTF-8 optimization, continuation efficiency, and type system fixes
group_summaries.new_features:
- Added [ocaml/ocaml#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14525) `Fun.todo` function to signal unimplemented functionality, with code location support and optional `todo` alert for detecting remaining placeholder code (merged by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole), closing long-standing [ocaml/ocaml#13072](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/13072))
- Improved [ocaml/ocaml#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14512) warning 16 (unerasable-optional-argument) detection to properly handle module-dependent function types (`Tfunctor`) introduced in OCaml 5.5 (merged by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob), fixing regression [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622))
- Optimized [ocaml/ocaml#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14620) UTF-8 lexeme processing in the compiler for better performance (merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- Made continuation objects more efficient by reducing from two fields to one in [ocaml/ocaml#14469](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14469) (backported to trunk via [ocaml/ocaml#14613](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14613))
- Fixed [ocaml/ocaml#14604](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14604) uncaught Cannot_apply exception when mixing polymorphic types and unboxed constructors (merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), fixing [ocaml/ocaml#14603](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14603))
group_summaries.group_overview:
- The core group saw focused activity exclusively in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) with 15 PRs merged and 14 new PRs opened during week 10
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no activity this week
- Primary focus areas included type system fixes for OCaml 5.5 regressions, runtime cleanup and domain management, performance optimizations, and continued work on complex type system refactorings
- 34 contributors participated in code review, issue reporting, and development across [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml)
- Multiple OCaml 5.5 regressions were identified and addressed, particularly related to the new `Tfunctor` type constructor introduced by modular explicits in [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622), [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630), and [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629)
group_summaries.activity_summary: Type system, GC, runtime, security, and performance work with 14 new PRs opened and 15 merged
group_summaries.activity:
- **Type System**: [ocaml/ocaml#14611](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14611) opened to keep expansion rebase well-founded, continuing work on long-standing [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/11648) (by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue))
- **Type System**: [ocaml/ocaml#14625](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14625) opened addressing recursive modules and module-dependent functions (by [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls))
- **Type System**: [ocaml/ocaml#14626](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14626) opened to fix handling of module-dependent functions in warning 16 (by [Alistair](https://github.com/johnyob))
- **Type System**: [ocaml/ocaml#14624](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14624) opened documenting Tfunctor FIXMEs needing attention (by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- **Garbage Collection**: [ocaml/ocaml#14610](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14610) opened to consume budget when marking ephemerons (by [Nick Barnes](https://github.com/NickBarnes))
- **Runtime**: [ocaml/ocaml#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14614) opened introducing fatal error when unjoined domains remain at cleanup-on-exit, addressing issues in [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) (by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- **Security**: [ocaml/ocaml#14615](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14615) opened to fix CVE-2026-28364 (by [Caleb Wolfe](https://github.com/xcawolfe-amzn))
- **Performance**: [ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14618) opened for faster unicode normalization in misc module (by [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel))
- **Documentation**: [ocaml/ocaml#14616](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14616) opened to document values given to MSVC prefetch intrinsics (by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA))
- **Tooling**: [ocaml/ocaml#14617](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14617) opened to make ocaml_deprecated_cli alert fatal in compiler development flags (by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron))
- **Error Messages**: Improved [ocaml/ocaml#14600](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14600) type parameter error messages to clarify they are bound in type declarations (merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron), addressing [ocaml/ocaml#6634](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/6634))
- **Code Cleanup**: Removed [ocaml/ocaml#14601](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14601) impossible unbound error path in extension constructor declarations (merged by [Florian Angeletti](https://github.com/Octachron))
- **Dead Code**: Removed [ocaml/ocaml#14623](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14623) dead Ctype.arity code (merged by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche))
- **CI Improvements**: Disabled [ocaml/ocaml#14612](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14612) in-prefix tests with --enable-tsan on Inria CI to avoid test failures (merged by [Olivier Nicole](https://github.com/OlivierNicole))
- **Jenkins**: Ensured [ocaml/ocaml#14608](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14608) Jenkins workers always clean up installation (merged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
- **Backports**: Cherry-picked [ocaml/ocaml#14613](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14613) continuation optimization from trunk (merged by [Damien Doligez](https://github.com/damiendoligez))
- **Backports**: Re-added [ocaml/ocaml#14609](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14609) missing commit from [ocaml/ocaml#14547](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14547) (merged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
- **Backports**: Fixed [ocaml/ocaml#14607](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14607) linking with libasmrun_shared.so on Risc-V for 4.14 branch (merged by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Runtime Cleanup**: Extensive discussion on [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) about segfaults and timeouts in parallel tests with cleanup-at-exit mode, exploring fundamental design questions about thread cancellation and resource cleanup, with follow-up PR [ocaml/ocaml#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14614) proposing fatal errors for unjoined domains
- **Feature Request**: [ocaml/ocaml#14627](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14627) proposing programmable patterns for OCaml to enable pattern matching on abstract types without exposing constructors, similar to Haskell's pattern synonyms and F#'s active patterns
- **Type System Regression**: [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622) reporting warning 16 incorrectly raised with GADTs and first-class modules in 5.5 (closed by [ocaml/ocaml#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14512), caused by modular explicits introducing `Tfunctor` type constructor)
- **Type System Regression**: [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630) about signature inference failure with recursive functions and first-class modules in 5.5, likely to be fixed by [ocaml/ocaml#14537](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14537)
- **Type System Regression**: [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629) about local open changing typechecker behavior for optional argument elimination in 5.5
- **Performance**: [ocaml/ocaml#14621](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14621) identifying tests taking too long on AppVeyor CI (some over 185 seconds, including forbidden.ml at 185s)
- **Stdlib**: [ocaml/ocaml#14619](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14619) questioning why List.concat/flatten is not tail-recursive
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- Multiple OCaml 5.5 regressions identified related to modular explicits and the new `Tfunctor` type constructor in [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622), [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630), and [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629), highlighting need for systematic audits when introducing new type constructors
- Continued active work on the complex keep-expansion type system refactoring in [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/11648), with cross-review between [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)
- Focus on runtime cleanup and domain management issues, particularly around cleanup-at-exit mode and proper resource management in [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) and [ocaml/ocaml#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14614)
- Performance optimization work focusing on UTF-8 processing in [ocaml/ocaml#14620](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14620) and Unicode normalization in [ocaml/ocaml#14618](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14618)
- Security vulnerability addressed with [ocaml/ocaml#14615](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14615) fixing CVE-2026-28364

weekly_summary.group_overview:
- __RUMINANT:core__ The **OCaml compiler** merged 15 PRs and opened 14 new ones, with 34 contributors addressing type system fixes for OCaml 5.5 regressions, runtime improvements, and GC enhancements in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ The **OxCaml group** delivered 48 merged PRs with focused work on backend optimization including register allocation improvements, unboxed types API refinement, and reaper robustness enhancements across [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **tooling ecosystem** saw package management improvements in [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) with tar extraction fixes and Windows cache enhancements, plus new LSP refactoring capabilities in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) including range formatting and type location queries
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ The **OCaml.org website** received exercise page bug fixes and continued governance calendar development in [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org)
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ No activity reported in multicore-specific repositories this week
- **Release activity**: [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) published version 3.22.0~alpha0 on March 2nd by [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder)
- Multiple OCaml 5.5 regressions were identified in __RUMINANT:core__ related to the new `Tfunctor` type constructor introduced by modular explicits, documented in [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622), [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630), and [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629)

weekly_summary.emerging_trends:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **OCaml 5.5 regression identification** as multiple issues surfaced related to modular explicits and the new `Tfunctor` type constructor in [ocaml/ocaml#14622](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14622), [ocaml/ocaml#14630](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14630), and [ocaml/ocaml#14629](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14629), highlighting need for systematic audits when introducing new type constructors
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Complex type system refactoring** continues with active work on keep-expansion implementation in [ocaml/ocaml#11648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/11648) with cross-review between [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) and [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel)
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Runtime cleanup and domain management** focus emerged with discussion on cleanup-at-exit mode and proper resource management in [ocaml/ocaml#14594](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14594) and [ocaml/ocaml#14614](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/pull/14614)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Backend performance optimization** series targeting elimination of unnecessary operations including sign extensions in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5535](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5535) and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5550](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5550), redundant register spills in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5514](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5514), and improved bounds checking in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5328](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5328)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Unboxed types API maturation** with refinement of primitive APIs using explicit %unsafe_array_idx in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5556](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5556) and mode-crossing fixes in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5564](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5564)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Reaper robustness improvements** through systematic bug fixing including error handling in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5547](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5547), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5540](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5540), and [oxcaml/oxcaml#5549](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5549), plus type rewriting in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5391](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5391)
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Static evaluation infrastructure** development with new compilation pipeline (tlambda→slambda→rawlambda) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5430](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5430) and follow-up slambda fracturing in [oxcaml/oxcaml#5544](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5544)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Package management robustness** continues as focus with comprehensive work on archive extraction, lock file handling, and Windows cache improvements across multiple Dune PRs by [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Long-standing bug resolution** with closure of Dune issues from 2022-2024 including infinite loop in cycle checking in [ocaml/dune#5771](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/5771), OpenBSD tar compatibility in [ocaml/dune#10123](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/10123), and relative path handling in [ocaml/dune#9564](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/9564)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Custom LSP request expansion** continues with new specialized extensions for type location in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1584](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1584) and code refactoring in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1545](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1545)
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OCaml 5.5 preparation** gaining momentum across tooling ecosystem with support work in js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2195](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/pull/2195)) and requests for odoc updates in [ocaml/odoc#1401](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1401)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Exercise page improvements** gaining momentum with contributions addressing sorting and display issues in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3552](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3552) and [ocaml/ocaml.org#3544](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3544)
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ **Governance infrastructure development** expanding with calendar-based features for platform team meetings including ICS scraping in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3550](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3550) and iCal export in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3536](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/pull/3536)
